info.name: MobileNumberValidation — MDMS Config
info.description:
Create and manage common-masters.MobileNumberValidation schema and data in MDMS v2.

Flow:
1. Set collection variables: url, username, password, userLoginTenant, stateTenant.
2. Run '1. Authentication / Get Auth Token' — stores authToken + userReqInfo automatically.
3. Run '2. Schema / Create Schema' once per environment.
4. Run the '3. Data / Create Data' requests for the countries you need.
5. Use 'Search Data' to verify, 'Update Data' to activate/deactivate records.

variable.description: Base URL — no trailing slash. Change to your deployment host.
MDMS admin username (must have MDMS_ADMIN or SUPERUSER role)
Tenant used for login (usually state tenant)
State-level tenant — schemas and MobileNumberValidation data live here
City-level tenant (not needed for MobileNumberValidation, kept for reference)

item.name: 1. Authentication
item.description: Run this first. Stores authToken and userReqInfo as collection variables for all subsequent requests.

exec: console.log('authToken and userReqInfo stored in collection variables.');

request.description:
Authenticates and stores authToken + userReqInfo as collection variables.

On success the test script captures access_token and UserRequest from the response and saves them. All subsequent MDMS requests use {{authToken}} and {{userReqInfo}}.

item.name: 2. Schema
item.description: Register the schema definition in MDMS v2. Run 'Create Schema' once per environment. If the schema already exists you will get a conflict — safe to ignore, verify with 'Search Schema'.

request.description:
Register the common-masters.MobileNumberValidation schema.

Fields:
- countryCode (string, required, unique key) — e.g. '+251'
- mobileNumberRegex (string, required) — full-anchor regex, e.g. '^[79][0-9]{8}$'
- default (boolean) — true for the active deployment config
- isActive (boolean) — false to hide a record from validation hooks

Run once per environment. A conflict error means the schema already exists — verify with 'Search Schema'.
Verify the schema exists and inspect its field definitions.

item.name: 3. Data
item.description: Create and manage MobileNumberValidation records. Each record is one country's config. Only one record should have default: true at a time.

request.description:
Seed Ethiopia mobile config.

Country code: +251
Pattern: ^[79][0-9]{8}$ (9 digits, starting with 7 = Safaricom ET, 9 = Ethiotelecom)
default: true — this is the active config for the deployment
isActive: true

Seed India mobile config.

Country code: +91
Pattern: ^[6-9][0-9]{9}$ (10 digits, starting with 6-9)
default: false, isActive: false — inactive unless this deployment uses Indian numbers.

To activate: run 'Update Data', set isActive: true and default: true (and update the previously active record to default: false).

Seed Kenya mobile config.

Country code: +254
Pattern: ^[17][0-9]{8}$ (9 digits, starting with 1 = Airtel, 7 = Safaricom)
default: false, isActive: false — inactive unless this deployment uses Kenyan numbers.

List all MobileNumberValidation records.

The response contains full records including id, uniqueIdentifier, and auditDetails — copy these values into 'Update Data' when you need to modify a record.

Search using MDMS v1 format — the same format used by digit-ui-esbuild's useCustomMDMS hook.

Response path: MdmsRes["common-masters"].MobileNumberValidation[]

This is useful to verify exactly what the frontend hooks receive.

Update an existing MobileNumberValidation record.

Steps:
1. Run 'Search Data (MDMS v2)' first.
2. From the response, copy:
   - id (UUID of the record)
   - uniqueIdentifier (the countryCode value, e.g. "+251")
   - auditDetails (createdBy, createdTime, lastModifiedBy, lastModifiedTime)
3. Edit the data object with your changes.
4. Replace the placeholder values in this request and send.

Common use cases:
- Activate a country: set data.isActive: true, Mdms.isActive: true
- Deactivate a country: set data.isActive: false, Mdms.isActive: false
- Change default: set data.default: true on the new default, then update old default to data.default: false
- Fix regex: update data.mobileNumberRegex to new pattern

Note: auditDetails is mandatory for updates — the request will be rejected without it.
